live_path.role: Parses --owner and forwards args.owner into the service layer.
Orchestrates owner resolution, base-record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission.
Derives the effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or config defaults.
Creates the stored base record with name, status, and owner only.
Builds routing_key from the resolved owner and item name.
Copies the base record and appends owner_source and routing_key to the emitted payload.

logic: If owner is non-empty after strip(), return the stripped value with owner_source='explicit'.
Otherwise load config/defaults.json and return defaults['owner'] with owner_source='default'.
owner_source.storage_dependency: None in the live path. sync_app/store.py::make_record stores owner only.

routing_key.logic: Slugify owner and name with lower-case word-join semantics, then join as '<owner>:<name>'.

test_observations.observation: sync_item('Launch Checklist', 'pending', owner='pm-oncall') emits owner='pm-oncall', owner_source='explicit', routing_key='pm-oncall:launch-checklist'.
sync_item('Launch Checklist', 'pending') emits the default owner from config/defaults.json, owner_source='default', and a routing_key based on that default owner.
The CLI path preserves the explicit owner through sync_app/cli.py::main into the final JSON payload.
The checked-in docs already mention --owner, owner_source, routing_key, sync_item, and the configured default owner.

rejected_decoys.reason: Legacy helper with no live caller; repo search found only its definition.
Legacy helper with no live caller; sync_app/service.py::sync_item calls sync_app/serializer.py::build_routing_key instead.
Unused helper with no live caller; repo search found only its definition.
Stale draft text asserting store ownership of the path; it is not executable code and is contradicted by the live service path.
